# Rollback Safety Checklist: SPEC-001 Foundation Migrations

**Purpose**: Validate whether the SPEC-001 requirements and plan define manual rollback coverage, sequencing, and safety constraints clearly enough for implementation and review.
**Created**: 2026-04-25
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` workflow based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Do the requirements require one checked-in rollback SQL file for every SQL-changing step from M53 through M61, including the `facility` seed, rather than only for schema-only migrations? [Completeness, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-015, Spec section SC-005]
- [x] CHK002 Do the requirements make manual rollback operator-driven, with no automated rollback CLI, `Migration.down()`, or other reverse runner entering SPEC-001 scope? [Completeness, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-016, Plan section Technical Context, Plan section Phase 0 Research Decisions]
- [x] CHK003 Do the requirements distinguish rollback expectations for column-backed migrations, table-backed migrations, and the `facility` seed strongly enough that implementers know when SQLite rebuild guidance, guarded table/index drops, and safe seed deletion rules apply? [Completeness, Spec section FR-016, Spec section SC-005]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK004 Is the required rollback order stated unambiguously as reverse execution from M61 through M53 rather than a generic "undo in reverse" instruction? [Clarity, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section User Story 3, Spec section FR-016, Plan section Verification Strategy]
- [x] CHK005 Is the pre-rollback database snapshot requirement explicit enough that operators cannot mistake rollback rehearsal for an in-place destructive action? [Clarity, Spec section User Story 3, Spec section FR-016, Spec section Assumptions]
- [x] CHK006 Is idempotent reverse SQL defined clearly enough to cover partial rollback and already-reversed state where practical, rather than only a clean one-shot rollback path? [Clarity, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section User Story 3, Spec section FR-015]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK007 Do the spec and plan stay consistent that rollback is a checked-in SQL artifact plus runbook concern, while the runtime migration chain remains forward-only? [Consistency, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-016, Plan section Technical Context, Plan section Phase 0 Research Decisions]
- [x] CHK008 Do the rollback requirements stay aligned with the constitution's additive-migration policy, including companion rollback files and the operator runbook? [Consistency, Spec section FR-015, Spec section FR-016, Constitution section VII]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK009 Are the rollback success criteria measurable enough to review coverage for all M53-M61 rollback artifacts and the required runbook instructions without inferring missing requirements from implementation details? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec section SC-005]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK010 Do the requirements cover rollback after a partial prior reversal, not just rollback from a fully applied forward migration? [Coverage, Spec section User Story 3, Spec section Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK011 Do the requirements cover both schema-object rollback safety and seed-data rollback safety, so reviewers can distinguish destructive table drops from conditional seed deletion? [Coverage, Spec section User Story 3, Spec section FR-015, Spec section FR-016]

## Notes

- Initial checklist pass identified one wording gap in the spec-level rollback requirements for migration-class-specific rollback behavior; this was remediated by expanding `FR-016` and `SC-005`.
